mission: Reduce the closed late-time IO eta target to exact standard thermal-leptogenesis targets without introducing new framework axioms.

assumptions: Hierarchical thermal leptogenesis with one effective lightest heavy Majorana species N1
Relativistic equilibrium initial abundance for N1
External source reduction Y_B = c_sph * eps1 * kappa_f * Y_N1^eq(0)
Davidson-Ibarra CP-asymmetry bound in the Standard-Model convention |eps1| <= 3 m_atm M1 / (16 pi v^2)

theorem_1_required_source_product.statement: Any standard hierarchical thermal-leptogenesis completion that reproduces the closed IO late-time eta must realize the exact product eps1 * kappa_f fixed here.

theorem_2_davidson_ibarra_floor_family.statement: If the Davidson-Ibarra bound is the active external CP-asymmetry ceiling, then the lightest heavy-neutrino mass must lie above the kappa_f-dependent floor given here.

theorem_3_poplawski_target_compatibility.statement: If a future source theorem identifies the previously fixed external freeze-out target T_f,target with the lightest heavy-neutrino scale M1, then Davidson-Ibarra saturation requires only the efficiency kappa_f given here; numerically this remains admissible and the Poplawski target scale lies far above the absolute Davidson-Ibarra floor.

final_boundary.safe_statement: No ambiguity remains in the standard hierarchical thermal-leptogenesis reduction of the closed late-time IO eta. What remains open is not the target numbers but the theorem sector that could generate them.
still_open_for_framework: an internal right-handed-Majorana or equivalent source sector
an internal washout-efficiency or freeze-out selector theorem
